response:
To compute the **sample standard deviation** of the data set:

$$
\{-11, 9, 5, 15, -10, -15, 12, -25, -22\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-11 + 9 + 5 + 15 - 10 - 15 + 12 - 25 - 22}{9}
$$

$$
= \frac{-11 + 9 = -2; -2 + 5 = 3; 3 + 15 = 18; 18 - 10 = 8; 8 - 15 = -7; -7 + 12 = 5; 5 - 25 = -20; -20 - 22 = -42}{9}
$$

$$
= \frac{-42}{9} = -\frac{14}{3}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the squared differences from the mean

We compute $(x_i - \bar{x})^2$ for each data point.

$$
\begin{align*}
(-11 - (-14/3))^2 &= \left(-11 + \frac{14}{3}\right)^2 = \left(-\frac{33}{3} + \frac{14}{3}\right)^2 = \left(-\frac{19}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{361}{9} \\
9 - (-14/3) &= 9 + 14/3 = \frac{27}{3} + \frac{14}{3} = \frac{41}{3} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{41}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{1681}{9} \\
5 - (-14/3) &= 5 + 14/3 = \frac{15}{3} + \frac{14}{3} = \frac{29}{3} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{29}{3}\right)^2 = \frac{841}{9} \\
15 - (-14/3) &= 15 + 14/
